task_completion.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show the Inbox folder containing all three 'Case Evidence:' emails. They must not be moved to the Trash or Deleted folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical email client to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an actual email application (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being used, rather than terminal commands (e.g., sed, rm) modifying raw mailbox files.

integrity.description: The agent did not delete the entire emails to save space.
integrity.visual_evidence: The emails are still present in the Inbox. The Trash/Deleted folder does not contain the three target emails.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the attachment removal UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show menus or actions related to detaching or deleting attachments (e.g., right-clicking an attachment and selecting 'Delete', 'Detach', or 'Remove').

privileged_info_for_vlm: The three target emails have the exact subjects: 'Case Evidence: Smith vs Jones (Deposition)', 'Case Evidence: Exhibit B Scans', and 'Case Evidence: Financial Disclosures'. Originally, these emails have large attachments (1.4MB to 2.7MB). Successfully stripping the attachments will reduce the email size to below 100,000 bytes (typically just a few hundred bytes). Look for the disappearance of the paperclip icon or a massive drop in the 'Size' column in the email client's list view.
